segments.text: Isn’t a man forced to labor on earth? Aren’t his days like the days of a hired hand?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As a servant who earnestly desires the shadow, as a hireling who looks for his wages,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: so I am made to possess months of misery, wearisome nights are appointed to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
When I lie down, I say,‘When will I arise, and the night be gone?’
I toss and turn until the dawning of the day.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust. My skin closes up, and breaks out afresh.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh remember that my life is a breath. My eye will no more see good.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The eye of him who sees me will see me no more. Your eyes will be on me, but I will not be.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As the cloud is consumed and vanishes away, so he who goes down to Sheol[^5] will come up no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He will return no more to his house, neither will his place know him any more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
“Therefore I will not keep silent. I will speak in the anguish of my spirit. 
I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Am I a sea, or a sea monster, that you put a guard over me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I say, ‘My bed will comfort me. My couch will ease my complaint,’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then you scare me with dreams and terrify me through visions,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: so that my soul chooses strangling, death rather than my bones.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
I loathe my life. I don’t want to live forever. 
Leave me alone, for my days are but a breath.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What is man, that you should magnify him, that you should set your mind on him,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that you should visit him every morning, and test him every moment?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How long will you not look away from me, nor leave me alone until I swallow down my spittle?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
If I have sinned, what do I do to you, you watcher of men? Why have you set me as a mark for you, 
so that I am a burden to myself?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Why do you not pardon my disobedience, and take away my iniquity? For now will I lie down in the dust. 
You will seek me diligently, but I will not be.
segments.speaker: Job
